border-style: solid; border-color: rgba(128, 128, 128, 0.2); } analyzing emulation results i'm now diving into the emulation results and making the mental shift to use the designated tool - grep_search - exclusively. my emulation script yielded numerous elements. i am currently sifting through these results to isolate the relevant data. investigating execution context i'm now investigating the execution context. the "run" text i was seeking isn't in the expected dom elements of workbench.html. it appears these elements are just my command text, not the prompt. the run alt+enter is absent. my agent chat is probably in an iframe! cdpevaluate on the main document won't work on the iframe, and i need to figure out how antigravity-auto-run-pro worked before. the webview guard uses document.queryselector('.react-app-container') || document.queryselector('[class*="agent"]') to see the isolated panel. identifying iframe targets i've just had a breakthrough. i understand now that the agent panel, being an isolated chromium process, appears as an iframe target in cdp. my focus now is on confirming if cdpgetpages(port) returns these iframe targets, and if /json/list includes them. i'm reviewing the previous output of /json/list to find the iframe target type, and determine if it includes the needed websocketdebuggerurl. discovering agent target i now realize the /json/list endpoint often excludes iframe targets by default, so i've been missing the agent webview. this could be because the "launchpad" title, not the "agent manager," and because opening a project might spawn a new agent panel altogether, an oopif in workbench.html. i've shifted my focus to retrieving all targets via the browser target, invoking target.gettargets. re-evaluating target retrieval i'm now re-evaluating target retrieval, remembering that the chat iframe wasn't always hidden. i'm querying /json/list again, but also invoking target.gettargets to retrieve all targets. my focus is on determining if /json/list changed with an update to hide iframe targets or if the chat is now categorized as such. previous versions of vs code used to list iframe targets via /json/list. now, the chat might be an iframe i need to find with target.gettargets!

border-style: solid; border-color: rgba(128, 128, 128, 0.2); } analyzing emulation results i'm now diving into the emulation results and making the mental shift to use the designated tool - grep_search - exclusively. my emulation script yielded numerous elements. i am currently sifting through these results to isolate the relevant data. investigating execution context i'm now investigating the execution context. the "run" text i was seeking isn't in the expected dom elements of workbench.html. it appears these elements are just my command text, not the prompt. the run alt+enter is absent. my agent chat is probably in an iframe! cdpevaluate on the main document won't work on the iframe, and i need to figure out how antigravity-auto-run-pro worked before. the webview guard uses document.queryselector('.react-app-container') || document.queryselector('[class*="agent"]') to see the isolated panel. identifying iframe targets i've just had a breakthrough. i understand now that the agent panel, being an isolated chromium process, appears as an iframe target in cdp. my focus now is on confirming if cdpgetpages(port) returns these iframe targets, and if /json/list includes them. i'm reviewing the previous output of /json/list to find the iframe target type, and determine if it includes the needed websocketdebuggerurl. discovering agent target i now realize the /json/list endpoint often excludes iframe targets by default, so i've been missing the agent webview. this could be because the "launchpad" title, not the "agent manager," and because opening a project might spawn a new agent panel altogether, an oopif in workbench.html. i've shifted my focus to retrieving all targets via the browser target, invoking target.gettargets. re-evaluating target retrieval i'm now re-evaluating target retrieval, remembering that the chat iframe wasn't always hidden. i'm querying /json/list again, but also invoking target.gettargets to retrieve all targets. my focus is on determining if /json/list changed with an update to hide iframe targets or if the chat is now categorized as such. previous versions of vs code used to list iframe targets via /json/list. now, the chat might be an iframe i need to find with target.gettargets!
